raw.objective: “to estimate the pharmacokinetic parameters of a population by using sparse data collected during routine clinical care. It can also be used to establish the extent to which patient characteristics influence the pharmacokinetics of the drug.”
raw.covariates: Tested: AGE, WT, Dose, SEX, Comedication(CBZ) Included: WT, DOSE, CBZDOSE, SEX, CO
raw.observations: The paper describes a simplified PK model. Results suggest a non-linear relationship between comedication of VPA +CBZ, where VPA CL decreases with increasing WT. CL in females was less than in males A nonlinear increase in VPA CL with increasing CBZ dose. is also suggested. Thus comedication causes an increase in VPA CL (10%).
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.equations: CL=(((theta1/1000)*(WT**theta2))*(DMGKG**theta3)*((CBZDOSE*WT)**theta4)*(theta5**(1-SEX))*(theta6**CO))*(1+eta1) CBZDOSE = theta7*exp(eta2) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) DMGKG: VPA dose (mg/kg/day) CBZDOSE: dose of carbamazepine (mg/kg/day) WT: total body weight (kg) SEX: gender (1:females, 0:males) CO: comedication (0:VPA+CBZ, 1:VPA+CBZ+’AED’)
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 6.06 theta2= 0.832 theta3= 0.414 theta4= 0.095 theta5= 0.943 theta6= 1.10 theta7=10.14 omega1= 0.0249 omega2=0.1303 sigma1= 0.0265

extraction_target.title: Detection of carbamazepine-induced changes in valproic acid relative clearance in man by simple pharmacokinetic screening.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL=(((theta1/1000)*(WT**theta2))*(DMGKG**theta3)*((CBZDOSE*WT)**theta4)*(theta5**(1-SEX))*(theta6**CO))*(1+eta1) CBZDOSE = theta7*exp(eta2) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) DMGKG: VPA dose (mg/kg/day) CBZDOSE: dose of carbamazepine (mg/kg/day) WT: total body weight (kg) SEX: gender (1:females, 0:males) CO: comedication (0:VPA+CBZ, 1:VPA+CBZ+’AED’)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “to estimate the pharmacokinetic parameters of a population by using sparse data collected during routine clinical care. It can also be used to establish the extent to which patient characteristics influence the pharmacokinetics of the drug.”

covariates.raw: Tested: AGE, WT, Dose, SEX, Comedication(CBZ) Included: WT, DOSE, CBZDOSE, SEX, CO

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
